subject: Biology
theory_html:
BIOLOGY — LIFE & EVOLUTION

CELL THEORY · GENETICS · EVOLUTION · MOLECULAR BIOLOGY

History of Biology

~350 BC
অ্যারিস্টটল / Aristotle — Classified 500+ animal species into systematic groups. Proposed spontaneous generation (life arises from non-living matter — e.g. flies from meat). Believed for ~2,000 years until Pasteur disproved it in 1859.

1665
রবার্ট হুক / Robert Hooke — Observed thin slices of cork under a compound microscope. Named the tiny compartments "cells" because they resembled monks' rooms (cellulae). Published Micrographia — first scientific bestseller. Van Leeuwenhoek (1674) observed live bacteria in pond water — first person to see living microorganisms.

1838–55
কোষ তত্ত্ব / Cell Theory — Schleiden (1838): all plants are made of cells. Schwann (1839): all animals too — unified all living things. Virchow (1855): "Omnis cellula e cellula" — all cells arise from pre-existing cells (no spontaneous generation of cells). These three principles remain the foundation of all biology.

1859
চার্লস ডারউইন / Charles Darwin — On the Origin of Species: Natural selection drives evolution. Organisms with advantageous traits survive and reproduce more (differential reproductive success). Over generations, populations change. All life shares common ancestry. Called "the most important book in science" and "the most dangerous idea in human thought."

1865
গ্রেগর মেন্ডেল / Gregor Mendel — 7 years of pea plant experiments (1856–63). Discovered laws of inheritance: Law of Segregation (alleles separate during gamete formation) and Law of Independent Assortment. Introduced dominant and recessive traits. His work was ignored for 35 years until rediscovered in 1900.

1953
ওয়াটসন ও ক্রিক / Watson & Crick — Discovered the double helix structure of DNA. Built on Rosalind Franklin's X-ray crystallography data (Photo 51). A=T, G=C base pairing means each strand is a template for the other — immediately suggesting how genetic information is copied. Awarded Nobel Prize 1962.

1983
PCR — Kary Mullis — Polymerase Chain Reaction: amplifies tiny DNA samples millions of times. Revolutionised forensic science, medicine, and virology. Won Nobel Prize 1993. Key technology behind COVID-19 PCR tests.

2003
মানব জিনোম প্রকল্প / Human Genome Project — Sequenced all 3.2 billion base pairs of human DNA. Found ~20,000–25,000 protein-coding genes (far fewer than expected). 99.9% of DNA is identical between any two humans. Opened era of personalised medicine and gene therapy.

Cell Theory & Cell Types

Three Pillars of Cell Theory: (1) All living organisms are made of cells. (2) The cell is the basic unit of life. (3) All cells arise from pre-existing cells (biogenesis).

আদিকোষী / Prokaryotic: No membrane-bound nucleus or organelles. DNA circular, in nucleoid region. Bacteria and Archaea. Size: 1–10 μm. Fastest reproducers (E. coli divides every 20 min). Came first on Earth (~3.5 billion years ago).

সুকোষী / Eukaryotic: True membrane-bound nucleus + specialised organelles. Size: 10–100 μm. Mitochondria (powerhouse — ATP via oxidative phosphorylation), Chloroplasts (photosynthesis), Ribosome (protein synthesis), ER, Golgi, Lysosome. Plants have cell wall (cellulose) + vacuole + chloroplasts.

DNA, Genetics & Heredity

DNA → Transcription → mRNA
mRNA → Translation → Protein
Codon: 3 nucleotides = 1 amino acid
Central Dogma: DNA makes RNA makes Protein. The genetic code is universal — same codons code same amino acids in all living things (strong evidence for common ancestry). 4 bases: Adenine(A), Thymine(T), Guanine(G), Cytosine(C). In RNA: Uracil(U) replaces T.

মেন্ডেলের সূত্র / Mendel's Laws: 1. Segregation: alleles separate during gamete formation (meiosis). 2. Independent Assortment: genes on different chromosomes sort independently. Punnett square predicts offspring ratios. Dominant (A) masks recessive (a). Heterozygous (Aa) shows dominant phenotype but carries recessive allele.

Photosynthesis & Respiration

6CO₂ + 6H₂O + light → C₆H₁₂O₆ + 6O₂
C₆H₁₂O₆ + 6O₂ → 6CO₂ + 6H₂O + ATP

Photosynthesis (in chloroplasts): Light reactions (thylakoid) split water, produce ATP + NADPH + O₂. Calvin cycle (stroma) fixes CO₂ into glucose using ATP + NADPH. Chlorophyll absorbs red (~680 nm) and blue (~430 nm) light, reflects green.

Aerobic Respiration (in mitochondria): Glycolysis → Pyruvate → Krebs cycle → Electron Transport Chain. Net yield: ~38 ATP per glucose molecule. Anaerobic: no O₂, yields only 2 ATP (lactic acid in muscles, ethanol in yeast).

Evolution & Natural Selection

5 Conditions for Natural Selection: (1) Variation exists in population. (2) Variation is heritable. (3) More offspring produced than can survive. (4) Individuals with favourable traits survive more. (5) Over generations → change in allele frequencies.

Evidence for Evolution: Fossil record (transitional forms), comparative anatomy (homologous structures), biogeography (related species on nearby islands), molecular biology (DNA similarity: humans share 98.7% with chimps, 60% with bananas). Speciation occurs when populations become reproductively isolated.
